method_atom: A gradient-force potential well, generated by an intense spatially-confined field mode, that traps mobile particles at the field-intensity maximum; the particles are transferred from one such potential well into a second well guided along a bounded hollow channel, with the fraction successfully loaded and later retrieved measured as a transfer efficiency.
object_atom: Ultracold sodium atoms moved from a free-space red-detuned optical dipole trap into a red-detuned gaussian light mode confined to the core of a hollow-core photonic crystal fiber, then retrieved outside.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is confinement of mobile particles in a guided-field potential minimum and quantified transfer between two such wells along a bounded channel; the specific ultracold-sodium-atoms-in-an-optical-fiber system is the domain-specific object.
